\rhead{\small Software \& Hardware Roadmap}

\title{\vspace{-2.0em}\textbf{RGCS v3 Software and Hardware\\
Architecture and Roadmap}}
\author{Andrew Green}
\date{2026}

\begin{document}
\maketitle

\begin{abstract}
The v3 platform layers a reusable typed mathematics library
(\texttt{rscs\_core}) under the deterministic crystal-application core
(\texttt{rgcs\_core}) and the desktop workbench, adds a synchronized
excitation/measurement architecture with one master timebase, and defines
an embedded platform contract (HG Embedded OS, \ENG). The Windows
portability debt of v2 is paid (POSIX bundle archive names, declared
dependencies, a two-OS CI matrix), persistence layers exist for crystal
records and Hydrogenuine memory, and the timing-hardware roadmap is
quantified before any exotic hardware is proposed: a TCXO reference is
mandatory, a DDS covers non-integer channels, a small CPLD is justified
only as a safety function, and an FPGA is explicitly \emph{not}
justified. All unbuilt hardware is \ENG{} until built and measured.
\end{abstract}

\section{Layering}
\texttt{rscs\_core} (typed coordinates/operators, registry, embedding,
firewall) is consumed by \texttt{rgcs\_core} (geometry, resonance,
anisotropy, optics, timing, drive, experiments, persistence, FEA export)
which is consumed by \texttt{rgcs\_desktop} (Qt panels as thin views over
headless, tested services). The mathematical provenance graph
(\gvGraphNodes{} nodes, \gvGraphEdges{} edges across the three machine
registries) is itself a generated artifact: every UI element that shows a
value shows its classification.

\section{Timing architecture}
One authoritative master timebase (\SI{32.768}{\kilo\hertz} TCXO
$\div\,8 \to$ \SI{4096}{\hertz} carrier) derives every channel; a channel
without a \emph{measured} latency calibration is phase-invalid (gate
H-29). Exact-cycle closures of the source keys \SRC{} are pure
arithmetic \DER:
\input{tables/timing_closures}
\input{tables/modulation_families}
The three macro envelope modes are frozen v2 definitions with cycle-exact
allocations; the source's ambiguous ``shorter by half'' statement maps to
the two distinctly named modes \texttt{half\_spacing} and
\texttt{double\_rate} (D7-002) --- neither is ever renamed.

\begin{figure}[H]
\centering
\includegraphics[width=0.9\linewidth]{macro_envelopes}
\caption{The three frozen macro envelope modes (dark = ON bursts, light =
final pause), rendered by the same code the desktop preview and firmware
acceptance tests use.}
\label{fig:macro}
\end{figure}

\section{Phase at the interaction coordinate}
The commanded phase is never the phase at the crystal. The platform
accumulates six declared terms --- cable, driver, inductive
$\arctan(\omega L/R)$, acoustic (oriented speed from the anisotropic
model, never a guessed scalar), optical transit, and group delay --- and
reports each separately. The interaction coordinate itself is a named
choice (geometric centre / predicted node / measured node), and coil
current, voltage, field, and phase are measured \emph{at the load}.

\section{Safety envelope}
Binding limits (D7-003): $\leq \gvVmax$~\si{\volt},
$\leq \gvImax$~\si{\ampere} peak, $\leq \gvEmax$~\si{\milli\joule} per
pulse, laser class $\leq$ 3R at $\leq \gvLaserMax$~\si{\milli\watt} with
interlock, specimen temperature-rise stop, and \emph{dummy-load-first}:
no operating point reaches a specimen before it is cleared on an
instrumented dummy load. There is no human-exposure or therapeutic
protocol anywhere in the programme, and function-generator presets are
signal-level recipes only.

\section{Embedded platform (HG Embedded OS) \ENG}
An ESP32/CYD-class board running FreeRTOS with a strict layer contract:
BSP (capabilities, no policy) $\to$ SDK (UI, storage, deterministic
timing, peripherals, logging, lifecycle) $\to$ one build-time-selected
app (RGCS controller, Karaoke migration as an SDK proof, self-test).
Safety limits compile into firmware --- SD-card configuration may tighten
but never loosen them; arming outputs requires a closed hardware
interlock loop, a passing boot self-test, and per-channel latency
calibration. The application manifest schema rejects any app requesting
an uncapped output class.

\section{Timing-hardware roadmap (quantified)}
Requirements first: jitter $\leq$ \SI{100}{\nano\second}~rms, phase
resolution $\leq 1^\circ$ at \SI{4096}{\hertz} (\SI{678}{\nano\second}),
drift $\leq$ \SI{2}{ppm}. Against these: ESP32 peripherals meet jitter
and resolution but its crystal fails drift, so the \textbf{TCXO
reference is mandatory}; an external \textbf{DDS} synthesizes the
non-integer channels the master-clock model flags (1496/644/587/20/21
\si{\hertz}); a \textbf{CPLD} is justified for the complementary coil
pair with hardware dead-time --- a safety function, not prestige; an
\textbf{FPGA is not justified} at these rates and is deferred until a
measurement requirement demonstrably exceeds the TCXO/DDS/CPLD chain.

\section{Portability and reproducibility}
v2's Windows defects are closed: bundle archive names are POSIX on every
platform (the checksum-verification failure class is regression-guarded),
and the ``specimen-listing defect'' proved to be an undeclared dependency
--- the honest lesson is that cross-platform reproducibility requires
\emph{declared} environments plus a CI matrix, which now runs Linux and
Windows across two Python versions with schema-validation and
generated-artifact freshness gates. One platform-documented exception
remains: byte-equality of Linux-generated golden CSVs is not portable
across platforms/library versions and is tolerance-checked instead
(tests remain byte-exact on the Linux reference platform).

\section{Implementation tranches}
T1 (complete): fixes, CI, persistence, FEA contract, headless services,
embedded contracts. T2: desktop panels over the tested services. T3:
FEA import scripts and analysis views. T4: firmware (BSP, timing
service, self-test). T5: timing hardware (TCXO/DDS/CPLD + interlocks;
\ENG{} until measured). T6: the H-29 latency-calibration campaign that
unblocks phase claims. Dependencies: T1$\to$T2$\to$T3 and
T1$\to$T4$\to$T5$\to$T6.

\section{Lessons learned (engineering)}
\begin{itemize}
\item Independent QA must be allowed to overturn the project's own
mathematics: the v2 coupling-map correction (anti-Hermitian $K = i 2\pi
g$) changed the physical interpretation and became a permanent
regression test.
\item Classification and provenance prevented source laundering:
historical operating points parameterize protocols as \SRC{} without
ever becoming evidence.
\item Frozen registries plus conservative-extension tests made a major
version safe: v3 provably cannot silently change v2.
\item Cross-platform reproducibility is a declared-environment problem
before it is a numerics problem.
\item The software and models advanced faster than the measurement
programme --- the strongest current output is a reproducible framework
and falsification plan, not experimental confirmation, and the platform
says so on every claim-bearing surface.
\end{itemize}
